title: Bit Digital Q3 2024 Financial Results: Revenue Surge and Strategic Cloud Gaming Deal
text: Bit Digital reported third-quarter 2024 revenue of $22.7 million, marking a 96% year-over-year increase. The company's high-performance computing (HPC) services division contributed $12.2 million in new revenue, while bitcoin mining generated $10.1 million representing an 11% decline from the prior year. The company secured a significant partnership with Boosteroid, the world's third-largest cloud gaming provider. The agreement represents potential revenue exceeding $700 million over a five-year period, with an initial order for 300 GPU servers projected to generate approximately $4.6 million in revenue. Bitcoin production fell 59% to 165.4 BTC during the quarter, primarily due to the April 2024 halving event and increased network difficulty. The company also faces capacity losses, as hosting partner Coinmint will discontinue 36 megawatts of service by early 2025. Bit Digital completed the acquisition of Enovum Data Centers for CAD $62.8 million (approximately USD $46 million), adding 4 megawatts of capacity with expansion potential exceeding 280 megawatts. The company maintained total liquidity of approximately $223.6 million as of September 30, 2024, with shareholders' equity reaching $315.0 million.
